sections.paragraph:
μέλισσα, ης, ἡ (cp. μέλι; Hom. et al.; PSI 426, 13; Ps.-Phoc. 127; JosAs; Philo in Eus., PE. 8, 11, 8; Jos., Ant. 5, 288; 6, 118) bee 6:6 (Ps 117:12).—B. 192. DELG s.v. μέλι.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Ps.-Phoc Ps.-Phoc , I/II a.d.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 627
